page: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It features 6 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, with 3 physical cores and 2 threads per core. The server is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 504.0 GB, yielding 84.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 1417 GB of local storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores reaching top-tier status in the top 10%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25%. However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 decompression, fall into the average category. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes are average, at 51.2 GB/sec and 36.19 GB/sec respectively.

The primary tradeoff of the FX24-6mds_v2 is its highly asymmetrical resource allocation. While it provides massive memory capacity, the low vCPU count limits parallel processing power. This is evident in LLM inference benchmarks, where text generation for a medium 7B model is poor and prompt processing for a large 70B model is weak. Qualitatively, this server offers high cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive memory footprints without the overhead of paying for unneeded vCPUs, but it is inefficient for compute-heavy parallel tasks.

This server is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, large-scale text processing, and workloads requiring substantial local storage alongside massive RAM. It is not recommended for heavy parallel computing or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server within the FXmdsv2 family, featuring 6 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is configured with an exceptionally high memory capacity of 504.0 GB (84.0 GB per core) and 1417 GB of local storage, with no GPUs. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core and text processing capabilities, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, its low core count results in average multi-core performance and poor results for complex parallel tasks like LLM text generation. This server provides excellent qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU scaling. It is best suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale text processing applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 6 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 6 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage, ideal for in-memory databases.
tagline: An ultra-high-memory server featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel processors and substantial local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 6 dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
504.0 GB of RAM providing 84.0 GB per core
1417 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
Limited parallel compute and LLM inference performance due to low core count
